_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify alleged violations of law, regulations, policy, or guidance
reference_document_element_description: Skill in interpreting regulatory text, supervisory guidance, and enforcement decisions, translating legal requirements into operational controls and measurable organizational obligations.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Skill RC-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-S-005 (RC, cosine: 0.595), RC-K-008 (RC, cosine: 0.584).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.0/10 (raw 0.295). Shared vocabulary: [guidance]. NICE-only terms [alleged, identify, law, policy, regulations, violations] vs WIDAI-only terms [controls, decisions, enforcement, interpreting, legal, measurable] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.880, R=0.860). P exceeds R by 0.020 — the NICE element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: entailment-dominant (91.0%). The model interprets "Identify alleged violations of law, regulations, policy, or guidance" as logically implying aspects of "Skill in interpreting regulatory text, supervisory guidance, and enforcement dec" — directional semantic containment consistent with 'Superset of' classification.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.598 > STS 0.295 (gap +0.303). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [guidance] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.7%. Shared tokens: [guidance]. NICE-only: [alleged, identify, law, policy, regulations, violations]. WIDAI-only: [controls, decisions, enforcement, interpreting, legal, measurable].

signals.detail: STS 0.295 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.303 with low STS 0.295. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
